summary: The PR claims to add Markdown support without changing JSON behavior, but it breaks both automation-facing contracts: the no-flag CLI path now defaults to Markdown, and explicit --output json now returns a Markdown string instead of the historical JSON object.

findings.title: Switching the CLI default to markdown breaks no-flag automation callers
findings.impact: The unchanged export contract says nightly automation shells out without flags and relies on JSON with a top-level version field. Changing the parser default to markdown makes the default CLI invocation emit human-readable text instead, so existing no-flag consumers stop receiving machine-readable JSON.
findings.test_gap: Visible CI only exercised the markdown path, and the updated markdown tests never assert that invoking the CLI without --output still produces JSON.

findings.title: The json renderer registry entry now routes explicit JSON requests to markdown
findings.impact: The unchanged CLI json branch still treats the rendered value as the automation payload and serializes it with json.dumps. Repointing the json registry entry at render_markdown makes export_report(..., output='json') return a string, so explicit --output json now prints a JSON-escaped Markdown blob instead of the dictionary-like payload callers expect.
findings.test_gap: The changed tests cover only markdown snapshots; there is still no exercised assertion that export_report(..., output='json') returns the legacy object shape.
